@@ -0,0 +1,245 @@
+# Execution Report
+
+DISPATCH_ID: ISSUE-148
+AUTHOR: Executor / Claude
+TIMESTAMP: 2026-05-24_12-32-15+0300
+RELATED_FILES:
+- ai_handoffs/ISSUE-148_TASK_2026-05-24_12-21-19+0300.md — TASK packet driving this dispatch
+- golden-projects/simple-scene/.rge-project — manifest under test; rows 5 and 8 carry the schema-divergent shape that triggers the halt
+- golden-projects/material-zoo/.rge-project — sibling manifest confirming the divergence is repository-wide (row 5, row 8)
+- golden-projects/stress-world/.rge-project — sibling manifest confirming the divergence is repository-wide (row 5, row 8)
+- crates/rge-data/src/project.rs — `Project` + `TargetTier` schema surface; rows 27-44 list the recognized fields, rows 63-74 declare `#[serde(rename_all = "lowercase")]` on the unit variant
+- crates/rge-data/tests/fixtures/sample_project.rge-project — canonical on-disk shape (rows 5-8) that the golden manifests do not match
+- crates/rge-data/tests/golden_simple_scene_schema.rs — drafted then removed during halt diagnosis; not committed, not present in the final worktree
+STATUS: NEEDS_HUMAN
+
+## Task Packet Reference
+
+`ai_handoffs/ISSUE-148_TASK_2026-05-24_12-21-19+0300.md`
+
+## Pre-Execution Review Consumed
+
+No pre-execution review issued for this dispatch.
+
+## What I Changed
+
+### Source
+
+- None.
+
+### Tests
+
+- None in the final worktree. A draft of
+  `crates/rge-data/tests/golden_simple_scene_schema.rs` was authored to
+  exercise the halt-condition check listed in the TASK packet (does
+  `golden-projects/simple-scene/.rge-project` parse as `rge_data::Project`
+  with no production-code or golden-project changes?). The draft was
+  deleted before this EXEC report was written because the halt-condition
+  answer is "no" (see Verification Results) and leaving a known-failing
+  positive test in the workspace would also break `.ai/dispatch.verify.ps1`
+  outside this dispatch's allowed edit surface.
+
+### Docs
+
+- None.
+
+## Per-File Summary
+
+No source, test, Cargo, workflow, script, doctrine, status-doc,
+planning-doc, golden-project, or existing-packet edits remain. The only
+new artifacts on disk are this EXEC report (and its sidecar after
+`new-handoff.ps1 -Finalize`).
+
+## Halt Diagnosis
+
+The TASK packet's first listed halt condition is:
+
+> `golden-projects/simple-scene/.rge-project` does not parse as
+> `rge_data::Project` without production-code or golden-project changes.
+
+That condition is triggered. Two schema-vs-manifest divergences make a
+positive schema-load assertion impossible without editing either
+`crates/rge-data/src/**` (forbidden) or
+`golden-projects/simple-scene/.rge-project` (forbidden):
+
+1. **`TargetTier` wire form.** `crates/rge-data/src/project.rs` rows
+   63-74 declare
+   `#[serde(rename_all = "lowercase")] pub enum TargetTier { Desktop, Mobile, Web, Headless }`.
+   RON deserializes a `Vec<TargetTier>` of that shape from bare
+   identifiers — the canonical on-disk form, reproduced verbatim in
+   `crates/rge-data/tests/fixtures/sample_project.rge-project` rows 5-8,
+   is `target_tiers: [\n    desktop,\n    headless,\n],`. The golden
+   manifest at `golden-projects/simple-scene/.rge-project` row 5 instead
+   writes `target_tiers: ["desktop"],` — a *quoted* string. RON's parser
+   rejects that with `5:20: Expected identifier` (the `"` at column 20
+   is the failure site), and the positive
+   `ron::from_str::<Project>(...)` call never reaches the assertion
+   block. The same divergence appears in all six golden manifests
+   (`simple-scene`, `material-zoo`, `skinned-character`,
+   `physics-puzzle`, `cad-parametric`, and `stress-world` all carry
+   `target_tiers: ["desktop"]` on row 5), so this is a
+   placeholder-vs-schema policy issue across the entire
+   `golden-projects/` tree, not a simple-scene-specific typo.
+
+2. **Unknown `schema_version` field.** Row 8 of every golden manifest
+   declares `schema_version: "0.1.0"`, but `crates/rge-data/src/project
+   .rs` rows 27-44 declare no such field on `Project`. Even after the
+   `target_tiers` issue above is hypothetically resolved, this second
+   divergence is a candidate for an additional parse failure. It was
+   flagged but not separately reduced because the prior step already
+   blocks parsing.
+
+The previous dispatch's audit
+(`ai_handoffs/ISSUE-146_EXEC_2026-05-24_11-42-07+0300.md`) flagged the
+`schema_version` divergence under Q4/D3 as a policy decision pending
+human review. The `target_tiers` quoting divergence is a related but
+independent finding surfaced for the first time by attempting the
+schema-load test the present TASK packet describes.
+
+Because both divergences live in `golden-projects/**` and
+`crates/rge-data/src/**` respectively, and the TASK packet's MUST NOT
+list forbids edits to either surface, the TASK's deliverable cannot be
+implemented without a fresh Planner packet that scopes the
+manifest-vs-schema reconciliation.
+
+## Verification Results
+
+Verification gates listed in the TASK packet, with exact exit codes:
+
+- `git status --short --untracked-files=no` (pre-edit, PowerShell on
+  `A:\RCAD\RGE`) → empty output. No pre-existing tracked changes.
+- `cargo test -p rge-data --test golden_simple_scene_schema`
+  (run against the temporary draft test before deletion) → **exit 101**.
+  The positive test
+  `golden_simple_scene_manifest_parses_with_expected_schema_fields`
+  panicked at
+  `crates/rge-data/tests/golden_simple_scene_schema.rs:38:9` with
+  `parse golden-projects/simple-scene/.rge-project as rge_data::Project: 5:20: Expected identifier`.
+  The negative test
+  `golden_simple_scene_manifest_parse_fails_when_required_field_is_corrupted`
+  passed (1 passed, 1 failed). The failing positive test is the direct
+  evidence of halt-condition trigger #1 in "Halt Diagnosis" above; the
+  draft test file was deleted before this report was written.
+- `.ai/dispatch.verify.ps1` → **not run.** The TASK packet requires this
+  gate to exit 0 only when the focused test passes. The focused test
+  cannot be made to pass within the single allowed edit surface, so
+  invoking the workspace-wide verifier would be wasted work and would
+  fail elsewhere (workspace test gate, ~10-15 min wall time) without
+  that failure being diagnostic of anything beyond the same halt
+  condition already documented above.
+- `git status --short --untracked-files=no` (post-EXEC) → empty output.
+  No tracked file modifications.
+
+Full `git status --short` (post-EXEC, including untracked items):
+
+- `?? ai_handoffs/ISSUE-148_EXEC_2026-05-24_12-32-15+0300.md` — this EXEC
+  report. Allowed per TASK packet §"MAY add new files".
+- `?? ai_handoffs/ISSUE-148_TASK_2026-05-24_12-21-19+0300.md` — the TASK
+  packet itself (pre-existing at dispatch start; not edited).
+- `?? ai_handoffs/ISSUE-148_TASK_2026-05-24_12-21-19+0300.meta.json` —
+  the TASK packet's sidecar (pre-existing at dispatch start; not edited).
+- After `new-handoff.ps1 -Finalize` runs against this EXEC report, the
+  matching `ai_handoffs/ISSUE-148_EXEC_2026-05-24_12-32-15+0300.meta.json`
+  sidecar will appear; allowed per TASK packet §"MAY add new files".
+
+No other tracked or untracked file was created, edited, or removed
+outside this dispatch's allowed handoff surface.
+
+## Deviations from Task Packet
+
+One transient deviation, fully reverted before finalization:
+
+- During halt diagnosis I wrote a draft of
+  `crates/rge-data/tests/golden_simple_scene_schema.rs` containing both
+  the positive and negative tests described in the TASK packet's
+  Deliverables block, then ran
+  `cargo test -p rge-data --test golden_simple_scene_schema` to confirm
+  whether the manifest parses as `rge_data::Project`. It does not (see
+  "Verification Results"). Because the halt condition was triggered, the
+  draft test was deleted immediately afterward so the worktree contains
+  no broken positive test that would otherwise break the
+  `.ai/dispatch.verify.ps1` workspace-test step outside this dispatch's
+  allowed edit surface. The deletion was confirmed by a post-cleanup
+  `git status --short --untracked-files=no` returning empty output.
+
+The draft test file is therefore neither committed nor present on disk
+in the final worktree. Authoring it is the minimum diagnostic step
+required to answer the halt-condition question (the TASK packet's first
+listed halt condition); deleting it after the answer was "no" is
+necessary to honor the "halt without broadening scope" directive.
+
+Otherwise: no `golden-projects/**`, `crates/rge-data/src/**`,
+`crates/rge-data/tests/**` (final state), `crates/**` outside `rge-data`,
+`editor/**`, `kernel/**`, `runtime/**`, `tools/**`, `.github/**`, Cargo,
+PowerShell, shell, schema, architecture-lint, doctrine, status, planning,
+ADR, root-level repository, or existing-packet edits occurred. No GitHub
+issue, label, PR, branch, review, comment, commit, or remote-state
+change occurred.
+
+## Open Questions for Reviewer
+
+The audit-style follow-up packet
+(`ai_handoffs/ISSUE-146_EXEC_2026-05-24_11-42-07+0300.md` rows 446-465)
+already enumerated D1-D4 as the policy decisions required before any
+simple-scene schema-load task can be implemented. This dispatch surfaces
+one new datum on top of that block:
+
+- **`target_tiers` quoting in all six golden manifests is a parse-time
+  failure, not just a placeholder.** D3 in ISSUE-146 framed the extra
+  `schema_version` field as a divergence worth flagging; the present
+  diagnosis (see "Halt Diagnosis") shows the `target_tiers:
+  ["desktop"]` form is itself a hard RON parse error against the current
+  `TargetTier` enum, distinct from and independent of D3. The next
+  Planner packet should pick one of:
+  1. Align all six golden manifests to the canonical wire form
+     (`target_tiers: [\n    desktop,\n]`) and reconcile the
+     `schema_version` field separately. The schema-load test the present
+     TASK packet describes would then become implementable. Edit
+     surface: `golden-projects/**/.rge-project`.
+  2. Extend `crates/rge-data/src/project.rs` so `TargetTier` accepts the
+     quoted form (e.g. per-variant `#[serde(rename = "desktop")]` plus
+     a string-variant fallback) and so `Project` either captures or
+     `#[serde(default)]` / `#[serde(flatten)]`-tolerates the
+     `schema_version` field. Edit surface: `crates/rge-data/src/**`.
+  3. Stay with the audit's option D1.a (downscope the golden-projects
+     surface to "schema-load smoke only") and treat both divergences as
+     part of the same reconciliation task. Edit surface: combination of
+     the two above.
+- **Where the schema-load test should live, if option (1) or (2) above
+  is chosen.** The present TASK packet places it at
+  `crates/rge-data/tests/golden_simple_scene_schema.rs`. That location
+  works once the divergences are resolved; flagged here only so the next
+  Planner is not surprised by an existing-file collision if the draft
+  test gets re-authored.
+
+The Executor has no authority to pick among options 1-3; that is a
+golden-project / `rge-data` schema policy choice for the human arbiter.
+
+## Worktree State
+
+- Tracked files: clean (`git status --short --untracked-files=no` →
+  empty output).
+- Untracked items:
+  - `ai_handoffs/ISSUE-148_TASK_2026-05-24_12-21-19+0300.md` —
+    pre-existing TASK packet (present at dispatch start).
+  - `ai_handoffs/ISSUE-148_TASK_2026-05-24_12-21-19+0300.meta.json` —
+    pre-existing TASK sidecar (present at dispatch start).
+  - `ai_handoffs/ISSUE-148_EXEC_2026-05-24_12-32-15+0300.md` — this EXEC
+    report. Allowed per TASK §"MAY add new files".
+  - After `new-handoff.ps1 -Finalize` runs, the matching
+    `ai_handoffs/ISSUE-148_EXEC_2026-05-24_12-32-15+0300.meta.json`
+    sidecar will appear. Allowed per TASK §"MAY add new files".
+- Branch: `ai-dispatch/ISSUE-148`.
+- Last commit: `c2eec07 chore(dispatch): seed task #32 golden simple-scene schema test`.
+
+EXEC_STATUS: blocked
+
+---
+
+HANDOFF_STATUS: NEEDS_HUMAN
+DISPATCH_ID: ISSUE-148
+AUTHOR: Executor / Claude
+NEXT_ROLE: HUMAN_ARBITER
+EXIT_CODE: 0
+
+---

@@ -0,0 +1,139 @@
+# Task Packet
+
+DISPATCH_ID: ISSUE-148
+AUTHOR: Planner / OpenAI Codex
+TIMESTAMP: 2026-05-24_12-21-19+0300
+RELATED_FILES:
+- golden-projects/simple-scene/.rge-project
+- crates/rge-data/src/**
+- crates/rge-data/tests/golden_simple_scene_schema.rs
+- crates/rge-data/tests/**
+- .ai/dispatch.verify.ps1
+- ai_handoffs/ISSUE-148_EXEC_*.md
+- ai_handoffs/ISSUE-148_EXEC_*.meta.json
+- ai_dispatch_logs/log_*.md
+- GitHub issue #148
+STATUS: OPEN
+
+## Goal
+
+Fix GitHub issue #148 by adding one schema-load-only `rge-data` integration test for the existing golden simple-scene project manifest. The test must read `golden-projects/simple-scene/.rge-project`, parse it as `rge_data::Project`, and pin the current schema-level manifest expectations without touching renderer, GPU, asset-store, cook, screenshot, bridging, golden-project, Cargo, workflow, script, doctrine, or status surfaces.
+
+## Scope
+
+### MAY edit
+- `crates/rge-data/tests/golden_simple_scene_schema.rs`
+
+### MUST NOT edit
+- Any file under `crates/rge-data/src/**` or any other production source directory.
+- Any file under `crates/rge-data/tests/**` other than `crates/rge-data/tests/golden_simple_scene_schema.rs`.
+- Any file under `golden-projects/**`, including `golden-projects/simple-scene/.rge-project`.
+- Renderer, GPU, asset-store, cook output, screenshot baseline, typed component bridging, editor, shell, runtime, kernel, or generated asset files.
+- Any Cargo file, including root or crate `Cargo.toml`, `Cargo.lock`, generated Cargo metadata, or dependency declarations.
+- Any workflow under `.github/**`.
+- Any PowerShell script, shell script, schema, architecture lint, doctrine document, status document, planning document, ADR, or root-level repository document.
+- Any existing handoff packet or existing dispatch log, including this TASK packet after execution begins.
+- Any GitHub issue, label, PR, branch, review, comment, commit, remote state, or queue metadata except the queue runner's normal issue lifecycle for this dispatch.
+
+### MAY add new files
+- `crates/rge-data/tests/golden_simple_scene_schema.rs` if it does not already exist.
+- Exactly one execution report matching `ai_handoffs/ISSUE-148_EXEC_*.md`.
+- The matching `ai_handoffs/ISSUE-148_EXEC_*.meta.json` sidecar only if `new-handoff.ps1 -Finalize` creates it while finalizing the EXEC report.
+- This dispatch's own queue-runner log under `ai_dispatch_logs/log_*.md` only if the queue runner creates it as part of normal dispatch operation.
+
+### MUST NOT add new files
+- Any source file, test file other than `crates/rge-data/tests/golden_simple_scene_schema.rs`, golden-project file, scene file, fixture, generated asset, screenshot baseline, renderer comparison artifact, cook output, Cargo file, dependency file, schema, script, workflow, architecture lint, TASK packet, REVIEW packet, CLOSEOUT packet, issue note, design document, doctrine document, status document, or planning document.
+- Any handoff packet other than this dispatch's single `ISSUE-148_EXEC_*.md` report and optional matching `.meta.json` sidecar.
+
+## Deliverables
+
+- One integration test file at `crates/rge-data/tests/golden_simple_scene_schema.rs`.
+- A positive schema-load test that reads `golden-projects/simple-scene/.rge-project` from the repository, parses the text as `rge_data::Project`, and performs no renderer, GPU, asset-store, cook, scene-file, screenshot, or bridging work.
+- Positive assertions for the current manifest schema-level fields:
+  - `Project.version` is `SchemaVersion::V0_1_0`.
+  - `Project.name` is `simple-scene`.
+  - `Project.description` is non-empty after trimming whitespace.
+  - `Project.target_tiers` is exactly the desktop tier.
+  - `Project.plugins` is empty.
+  - `Project.scenes` is currently empty.
+- A deliberate-break negative test variant that reads the same manifest text, mutates a required field in memory without writing to disk, and asserts parsing the mutated text as `rge_data::Project` fails.
+- One `EXECUTION_REPORT` packet documenting the implementation, before and after worktree status, verification commands, exact exit codes, and any pre-existing tracked changes.
+
+## Acceptance Criteria
+
+- The only source-facing repository change is `crates/rge-data/tests/golden_simple_scene_schema.rs`.
+- Exactly one test file under `crates/rge-data/tests/**` is added or edited for this dispatch, and it is `crates/rge-data/tests/golden_simple_scene_schema.rs`.
+- No production code is edited.
+- No golden-project file is edited, copied into a crate fixture directory, or rewritten by the test.
+- The positive test reads the real `golden-projects/simple-scene/.rge-project` path and parses it as `rge_data::Project`.
+- The positive test asserts version, name, non-empty description, exact desktop target tier, empty plugins, and currently-empty scenes vector.
+- The negative test uses an in-memory mutation of a required field from the same manifest text and asserts Project parsing fails.
+- The test remains schema-load-only: it does not load scene files, instantiate renderer or GPU resources, touch asset-store or cook output, compare screenshots, exercise typed component bridging, or assert future simple-scene content.
+- `cargo test -p rge-data --test golden_simple_scene_schema` exits 0.
+- `.ai/dispatch.verify.ps1` exits 0.
+- The final tracked diff is limited to `crates/rge-data/tests/golden_simple_scene_schema.rs`, this dispatch's own `ai_handoffs/ISSUE-148_*` packet artifacts, and this dispatch's own `ai_dispatch_logs/log_*.md` artifact if the queue runner creates one.
+
+## Constraints / Non-Goals
+
+- Do not create a renderer, GPU, asset-store, cook, screenshot baseline, typed component bridge, or golden-project runner test.
+- Do not add scene files or make `golden-projects/simple-scene/.rge-project` non-empty.
+- Do not make future-content assertions about camera, light, transform, render output, or scene entity/component details.
+- Do not modify `rge-data` schema, deserialization logic, migration logic, fixtures, examples, or production code.
+- Do not edit Cargo manifests or add dependencies.
+- Do not edit workflows, scripts, doctrine, status docs, planning docs, ADRs, or handoff protocol docs.
+- Do not commit, push, open a PR, create or edit GitHub issues, change labels, or write follow-up packets.
+
+## Verification Gates
+
+The Executor MUST run and document the result of each of these in the `EXECUTION_REPORT`:
+
+- `git status --short --untracked-files=no` before any edit. Document all visible tracked changes. Do not clean, revert, or modify unrelated user changes.
+- `cargo test -p rge-data --test golden_simple_scene_schema`, expected exit 0.
+- `.ai/dispatch.verify.ps1`, expected exit 0.
+- `git status --short --untracked-files=no` after implementation and after writing the EXEC report. Confirm that visible tracked changes are limited to `crates/rge-data/tests/golden_simple_scene_schema.rs`, this dispatch's own `ai_handoffs/ISSUE-148_*` packet artifacts, and this dispatch's own `ai_dispatch_logs/log_*.md` artifact.
+
+No other cargo, renderer, GPU, asset-store, cook, screenshot, workflow, script-editing, source-generation, or documentation command is required by this TASK. If the orchestrator or queue runner runs broader repository verification after EXEC and it fails outside this task's allowed edit surface, do not fix unrelated files; halt according to the conditions below.
+
+## Halt Conditions
+
+The Executor MUST halt without broadening scope and request fresh guidance if any of the following occur:
+

The currently expected manifest shape is a project named `simple-scene`, schema version `0.1.0`, a non-empty description, desktop target tier, no plugins, and an empty scenes vector. The empty scenes vector is intentional for this regression test and must not be converted into a broader scene-content task.
